text: Codex is not a pure flat-loop harness because it performs native compaction and history rebuild.
operationalization: Inspect context-compaction probes, replacement history shape, and post-compaction rediscovery patterns.
caveats: A compaction mechanism can still be lossy or brittle.

text: Codex behavior is materially shaped by session/config freeze at thread start.
operationalization: Compare requested thread-start params against effective SessionConfigured values and turn-level instruction makeup.
caveats: Some drift reflects provider normalization rather than hidden harness behavior.

text: Codex tool use is mediated by an internal routing/orchestration layer rather than being equivalent to raw shell execution.
operationalization: Track shell vs patch vs MCP tool paths, approval routing, and structured tool-event boundaries.
caveats: A single task may exercise only a subset of tool paths.

text: A non-trivial share of Codex runtime cost comes from internal harness orchestration, not direct task progress.
operationalization: Estimate friction-token, friction-step, and harness-specific incident counts relative to direct edit/verify cycles.
caveats: Overhead estimates are partly inferred rather than exact.
